content.text: How do I create a method to start a stopwatch and continuously update its last known execution time within a class?

This approach checks if the timer is currently inactive before recording the initial start time. It then records the current timestamp for tracking duration in every call.

```python
def start(self):
        """Start the timer."""
        if not self._is_running:
            self._t_start = time()
            self._is_running = True
        self._t_last = time()
```

The function successfully initializes `_t_start` only on the first run but updates `_t_last` regardless of whether it was already running or not.
If I call this method thousands of times per second, will the startup time (`_t_start`) change?
No, because of the initial check `if not self._is_running:`, only the first call sets `self._t_start` to prevent continuous resetting.
Got it! So this is a robust timing hook.
